hero.headline: AI Agents That Actually Work — In Real Environments
hero.subheadline: Self-hosted multi-agent AI orchestration platform. Run AI agent swarms in isolated containers. Full governance. Zero vendor lock-in.

features.title: Built for Production
features.description: Everything you need to run AI agents reliably at scale.

items.title: Container Isolation
items.description: Every agent runs in its own isolated container. No cross-contamination, no shared state, full security.

items.title: Orchestration Engine
items.description: Coordinate multi-agent workflows with structured handoffs, dependency graphs.

items.title: Scoped Memory
items.description: Agents maintain context within defined boundaries. Session memory, persistent memory, shared memory.

items.title: Pluggable Skills
items.description: Extend agent capabilities with skills. File operations, API calls, code execution, and custom actions.

items.title: Multi-Provider LLM
items.description: Anthropic, OpenAI, and more. Switch providers without changing agent logic. No vendor lock-in.

items.title: RBAC & Audit Logs
items.description: Role-based access control and comprehensive audit trails. Know who did what and when.

providers.title: Multi-Provider Support
providers.description: Switch providers without changing agent logic. No vendor lock-in.

channels.title: Multi-Channel Support
channels.description: Reach users wherever they are. One platform, every channel.

workspaces.title: Multi-User Support with Isolated Workspaces
items: Isolated workspaces per user or team
Separate agents, memory, and credentials per workspace
Full tenant isolation — no data leakage between users

security.title: Enterprise-Grade Security
security.description: Built for organizations that need governance and compliance.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Everything you need to know about Clawix.

items.question: What is Clawix?
items.answer: Clawix is an open-source, self-hosted multi-agent AI orchestration platform designed for secure AI workflow execution, governance, memory management, and scalable agent collaboration inside isolated Docker containers.

items.question: Who is Clawix built for?
items.answer: AI consultants building enterprise AI workflows, AI enthusiasts experimenting with autonomous agents, junior developers learning AI orchestration and infrastructure, and organizations requiring private and governable AI systems.

items.question: What makes Clawix different from other AI agent frameworks?
items.answer: Clawix focuses on production-grade orchestration with container-isolated agents, self-hosted deployment, governance and auditability, multi-agent collaboration, multi-provider AI support, persistent memory and workspaces, and role-based access control.

items.question: Is Clawix open source?
items.answer: Yes. Clawix is publicly available as an open-source project under the MIT license.

items.question: Which AI providers are supported?
items.answer: Available providers include Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Z.AI Coding, Kimi and OpenAI-compatible APIs. Planned providers include Gemini, DeepSeek, Azure OpenAI, and OpenRouter.

items.question: Can I use local LLMs with Clawix?
items.answer: Yes. Clawix supports OpenAI-compatible endpoints such as Ollama, vLLM, and self-hosted inference APIs.

items.question: Does Clawix require Docker?
items.answer: Yes. Docker is required because every AI agent runs inside its own isolated container for security, sandboxing, and resource management.

items.question: How do I install Clawix?
items.answer: Clone the repository, start Docker Desktop, and run: pnpm run install:clawix. The installer automatically configures environment variables, builds Docker images, and starts required services.

items.question: How are API keys and secrets protected?
items.answer: Provider API keys and secrets are encrypted using AES-256-GCM encryption and stored securely on the server.

items.question: What governance features does Clawix provide?
items.answer: Governance features include audit logs, token budgets, structured logging, Prometheus metrics, scoped memory controls, and role-based access control (RBAC).

items.question: What is the scoped memory system?
items.answer: Clawix supports private memory, group memory, and organization-wide memory. This enables AI agents to retain contextual knowledge across workflows and teams.

items.question: What features are planned for the future?
items.answer: Planned roadmap items include WhatsApp integration, Slack integration, skill marketplace, advanced token analytics, multi-region deployment, and additional AI provider integrations.

finalCta.title: Move from Experiment to Production
finalCta.description: Stop building AI demos. Start deploying AI systems.
